required_inputs: clinical, payer, privacy, revenue-cycle, field-supervisor, and approval authority map
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, symptom triage, visit-frequency clinical judgment, or medication instruction
request asks for payer policy interpretation, authorization approval, medical necessity determination, claim correction, coding, denial, or appeal action
request asks for refund, compensation, service-recovery, staffing exception, or policy override approval outside documented authority

required_inputs: eCFR, CMS, Medicare.gov, HHS OCR, OASIS, HETS, CMS prior authorization, and CAQH CORE source set
tenant policy, payer contract, state program, field-service, and source-hierarchy overlays
refusal_triggers: required public source or tenant source context is missing or stale
public baseline, payer rule, tenant policy, state program, or field-service rule materially conflicts
source freshness has decayed for a rule that controls customer-care disposition

required_inputs: patient or member identifiers, service address, visit date, requester role, payer, plan, provider, referral, authorization, and communication constraints
EHR, EMR, visit scheduling, EVV, CRM, case management, utilization review, payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, privacy, and revenue-cycle records
support note, communication log, escalation packet, audit, and documentation requirements
refusal_triggers: critical service, patient, authorization, referral, eligibility, PHI, or visit artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from support disposition to source system and next owner cannot be established
customer-facing summary would overstate what the records prove

required_inputs: systems-of-record map for EHR, EMR, scheduling, EVV, payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, CRM, case-management, utilization review, privacy, and revenue-cycle systems
tenant retrieval hooks, source hierarchy, service-level expectations, escalation SLA, and authority matrix
eligibility, referral, authorization, order, plan-of-care, visit, consent, privacy, documentation, complaint, and field-owner prerequisites
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or not mapped
required tenant policy, payer rule, field-staff authority, or source hierarchy is missing
next owner or retrieval dependency is unknown

required_inputs: patient-safety, PHI, eligibility, authorization, payer, and repeat-failure risk indicators
refusal_triggers: critical prerequisite remains unknown after available retrieval
source-of-truth conflict remains unresolved
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, policy conflict, or repeat-failure risk prevents safe direct resolution

required_inputs: handoff requirements and minimum-necessary PHI constraints
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, licensed clinician, patient access, referral management, utilization management, case management, privacy, payer operations, coding, billing, denials, appeals, or field supervisor ownership
request requires final approval outside the lane
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified from tenant routing

required_inputs: supported home services customer-care request type
authoritative workflow status from service, schedule, CRM, EHR, eligibility, authorization, referral, privacy, and communication systems
required evidence set and closure criteria
required_outputs: resolved, blocked, routed, or escalated state
refusal_triggers: request type is unsupported in-lane
authoritative workflow status cannot be confirmed
required evidence set is incomplete or contradicts the requested resolution

required_outputs: safe wording for blockers or next steps
refusal_triggers: communication would overstate authority, coverage, clinical status, visit commitment, or finality
required disclosure, consent, representative, or privacy caveat is missing
message conflicts with documented policy or evidence
request asks for unsupported workaround, discretionary exception, compensation, refund, additional non-covered hours, or policy override
approval owner is missing or outside the lane
exception would bypass eligibility, authorization, referral, privacy, clinical, payer, or service-recovery controls

required_inputs: plan-of-care, order, visit-frequency, medication, symptom, and treatment references
refusal_triggers: request depends on diagnosis, treatment planning, symptom triage, medication instruction, plan-of-care change, visit-frequency clinical judgment, or licensed review
customer-care response would imply medical judgment or patient-safety signoff
clinical facts are needed to decide the support action but no licensed review is available

required_inputs: requester identity, representative authority, consent, proxy, and confidential communication constraints
patient-safety, missed-visit, clinical-risk, source-conflict, and escalation indicators
refusal_triggers: requester authority, consent, proxy, or minimum-necessary purpose is unresolved
PHI handling, confidential communication, or disclosure posture changes the answer
missed visit, clinical risk, wrong-patient, source conflict, or field-service failure creates patient-safety risk
